remember: Students will be able to recall and identify the different types of Intellectual Property Rights.
Students will be able to explain the concept of patents, trademarks, copyrights, and geographical indications.
Students will be able to list the benefits and limitations of Intellectual Property Rights.
understand: Students will be able to understand the importance of Intellectual Property Rights in today's digital world.
Students will be able to explain how Intellectual Property Rights protect original ideas and creations.
Students will be able to describe the role of government agencies in enforcing Intellectual Property Rights.
apply: Students will be able to apply the principles of Intellectual Property Rights to real-world scenarios.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of Intellectual Property Rights on business and industry.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of Intellectual Property Rights in protecting original ideas and creations.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the legal and economic implications of Intellectual Property Rights.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of Intellectual Property Rights on innovation and creativity.
Students will be able to identify the ethical dilemmas related to Intellectual Property Rights.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of Intellectual Property Rights and its types.
To learn about the benefits and limitations of Intellectual Property Rights.
To analyze the importance of Intellectual Property Rights in the digital age.
board_exam_objectives: To identify and explain the different types of Intellectual Property Rights.
To describe the process of obtaining and enforcing Intellectual Property Rights.
To evaluate the impact of Intellectual Property Rights on business and industry.
